schema_output.narrative_text: Ḥāṭib, a trusted companion of the Prophet, traveled to Egypt to meet with the Byzantine ruler, al-Muqawqis. He presented a letter from the Prophet, seeking al-Muqawqis' aid in the struggle against the Quraysh.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A grand Egyptian throne room with marble walls in soft cream and pale blue hues, and a high vaulted ceiling adorned with intricate gold filigree. Late afternoon sunlight streams through large windows on the left, casting a warm glow across the room. In the center, a majestic throne sits on a raised platform, with the central figure seen entirely from behind, wearing a richly embroidered golden cloak and a crown, with a sceptre in his hand, and a look of seriousness on his face. Before him, Ḥāṭib, a slender man with slightly stooped shoulders, wearing a simple white thobe and a brown woolen cloak with a white cloth head covering, stands with a letter in his hand, presenting it to the central figure with a respectful gesture. Soft warm golden light, formal and dignified mood. Medium shot at eye level. Cool blue, cream, and gold palette with the golden cloak as a color anchor. No modern objects, historically accurate 7th century scene.
